title: quassia pumila
intro: Quassia pumila, also known as Quassia amara, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, particularly in the Caribbean and the Andes. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its bark and roots, which contain quassin, a unique compound with anthelmintic, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Quassia pumila include its ability to treat intestinal parasites, reduce inflammation, and alleviate digestive issues. Quassia pumila can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or powder for use in herbal medicine, although its use should be guided by a qualified healthcare professional due to potential interactions and side effects.
